channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: It’s springtime in Germany and people are obsessing about white asparagus
item.description: BEELITZ, Germany (AP) — Asparagus season has arrived in Germany, signaling the unofficial beginning of springtime. There is no other vegetable that Germans obsess about as much as white asparagus.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Contract for Hong Kong company to operate Panama Canal ports had irregularities, audit finds
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Panama’s comptroller authority said Monday an audit found irregularities in the renewal of a 25-year port concession in the interoceanic canal and it would request an investigation into the authorization of the contract to a Hong Kong company.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Kentucky watches for surging rivers to recede so widespread cleanup can begin
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — After days of unrelenting downpours swelled rivers to near record levels across Kentucky, residents closely monitored waterways for signs they had crested, but freezing temperatures forecast for Tuesday could complicate any cleanup efforts.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Scholarships for child care are drying up. Now families are paying the price
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — For parents who need to work but can't afford the steep cost of child care, federally funded scholarships can be a lifeline. Delivered through state child care assistance programs, the scholarships can mean the difference between a parent working full time — or not at all.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Ex-member of former Gambia dictator's military unit on trial for torture in US
item.description: Opening statements are expected Tuesday in the U.S. trial of a man accused of torturing political opponents of Gambia’s ex-military dictator Yahya Jammeh nearly 20 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Slevin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's DHS revokes legal status for migrants who entered the US on Biden-era CBP One app
item.description: MCALLEN, Texas (AP) — Migrants who were temporarily allowed to live in the United States by using a Biden-era online appointment app have been told to leave the country “immediately,” officials said Monday. It was unclear how many beneficiaries would be affected.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Clem Burke, multifaceted drummer of iconic rock group Blondie, has died
item.description: Clem Burke, whose versatile drumming propelled the iconic rock group Blondie during its decades of music that spanned from new-wave punk to disco-infused tunes, has died.
item.dc:creator: Hallie Golden, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How Philadelphia police draw on personal experiences to respond to mental health crises
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — On a frigid February morning, Vanity Cordero, a Philadelphia police officer, heard a call over the radio for a man threatening to jump from a bridge. The details sounded familiar.
item.dc:creator: CLAUDIA LAUER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says high tariffs may have prevented the Great Depression. History says different
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In the early days of the Great Depression, Rep. Willis Hawley, a Republican from Oregon, and Utah Republican Sen. Reed Smoot thought they had landed on a way to protect American farmers and manufacturers from foreign competition: tariffs.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Robert Caro, Salman Rushdie and Sandra Cisneros honored by Authors Guild
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Robert Caro, Salman Rushdie and Sandra Cisneros were honored Monday night at an Authors Guild dinner gala that celebrated the written word and its vital role in the preservation of democracy.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: RFK Jr. says he plans to tell CDC to stop recommending fluoride in drinking water
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — U.S. Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. on Monday said he plans to tell the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention to stop recommending fluoridation in communities nationwide.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Will Trump tariffs bring jobs back to the US? Economists are skeptical
item.description: Economists on both sides of the aisle are skeptical that Trump's tariffs will lead to more factory jobs in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, NewsNation

item.title: Swollen rivers are flooding towns in the US South after a prolonged deluge of rain
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — Days of unrelenting downpours swelled rivers to near record levels across Kentucky on Monday, submerging neighborhoods and threatening a famed bourbon distillery in the state capital.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump honors Dodgers' 2024 World Series championship team
item.description: (KTLA) -- President Donald Trump welcomed Shohei Ohtani, Mookie Betts and the reigning champion Los Angeles Dodgers to the White House on Monday to congratulate them for winning the World Series last season. "That is a very good-looking group of people," Trump said as he began his remarks.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KRON4

item.title: China threatens countermeasures after Trump threatens additional 50% tariff
item.description: China threatened Tuesday to take countermeasures against the United States "to safeguard its own rights and interests” in response to President Donald Trump’s threat of an additional 50% tariff on Chinese imports.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Johnny Gaudreau's wife Meredith announces birth of couple's third child
item.description: Johnny and his brother were killed in New Jersey when they were riding bicycles and struck from behind by a suspected drunk driver.
item.dc:creator: Orri Benatar, NewsNation

item.title: Seven GOP senators sign onto bill to check Trump's trade authority
item.description: The legislation would limit Trump’s ability to impose unilateral tariffs without the approval of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Harriet Tubman reference removed from National Parks' Underground Railroad web page
item.description: The National Parks Service (NPS) has removed a reference to abolitionist Harriet Tubman from its webpage dedicated to the Underground Railroad.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, ABC 4

item.title: Mega Millions tickets rise to $5, but the lottery promises more giant jackpots
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Mega Millions players will get slightly better odds and should start seeing more billion-dollar jackpots, but at a cost — literally — with tickets for the multistate lottery jumping in price to $5.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT McFETRIDGE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: VIDEO: 6-year-old found wandering alone with dog in Florida; mom, 1 other charged
item.description: The Volusia Sheriff's Office said a 6-year-old girl and her dog were found wandering alone on Friday afternoon in Deltona.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, KRON4

item.title: Trump says he's not backing down, calls tariffs 'medicine' as markets reel
item.description: Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One, Trump said he didn't want global markets to fall, but also that he wasn't concerned about the massive sell-off either.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump meets Netanyahu as first trade talks with world leaders begin and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jay North, TV's Dennis the Menace, dies at 73
item.description: North was 6 when he was cast as the smiling troublemaker in the CBS sitcom adaptation of Hank Ketcham's popular comic strip that took place in an idyllic American suburb.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pennsylvania cop shot by gunman involved in car crash, officials say
item.description: A Pennsylvania man was involved in a car crash Sunday before running from the scene and opening fire at police, wounding one officer, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Researchers developing at-home smell test to detect Alzheimer’s
item.description: A smell test is less invasive and more accessible than other methods like PET scans, lumbar punctures or blood tests.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5

item.title: 22,600 lbs of bratwurst recalled in 10 states over possible plastic contamination
item.description: Federal health officials say thousands of pounds of bratwurst sold in 10 states are being recalled because the sausages may contain hard plastic.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, Fox 5

item.title: Second child died from measles-related causes in West Texas
item.description: A second school-aged child in West Texas has died from a measles-related illness, a hospital spokesman confirmed Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Protesters from New York to Alaska assail Trump and Musk in 'Hands Off!' rallies
item.description: Crowds of people angry about the way President Donald Trump is running the country marched and rallied in scores of American cities Saturday in the biggest day of demonstrations yet by an opposition movement trying to regain its momentum after the shock of the Republican’s first weeks in office.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jaguar and Land Rover carmaker pauses shipments to US over Trump tariffs
item.description: The company that makes Jaguar and Land Rover vehicles said on Saturday it would pause shipments to the U.S., days after President Donald Trump’s tariffs took effect.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Tariffs will make sneakers, jeans and almost everything Americans wear cost more, trade groups warn
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Sending children back to school in new sneakers, jeans and T-shirts is likely to cost U.S. families significantly more this fall if the bespoke tariffs President Donald Trump put on leading exporters take effect as planned, American industry groups warn.
item.dc:creator: ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Al Green says he’ll present articles of impeachment against Trump in next 30 days
item.description: Green, who vowed to bring articles of impeachment against Trump roughly two months ago, argued that Trump does not “deserve the office” he holds.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Trump expected to host Netanyahu at White House next week
item.description: President Trump is expected to host Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu for a visit on Monday, a White House official told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: NY public schools tell Trump administration they won't comply with DEI order
item.description: New York state officials have told the Trump administration that they will not comply with its demands to end diversity, equity and inclusion practices in public schools, despite the administration's threats to terminate federal education funding.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Final Four again has multiple 5th-year starters. This time, it's a farewell to an uncommon era
item.description: “Credit to the NCAA for giving us a year back because that COVID year, that wasn’t real, honestly. Nah, it wasn’t fun at all.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: US must return Maryland father mistakenly deported to El Salvador prison, judge rules
item.description: A federal judge ordered the Trump administration to arrange for the return of a Maryland man after he was mistakenly deported to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Staff working on childhood lead exposure and cancer clusters fired from CDC
item.description: Staff members who fought childhood lead exposure and those who worked on cancer clusters were among those who were fired from the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS), a now former employee told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, ABC 4
